reasoning: The document has a CRITICAL programmatic defect due to pervasive header/footer elements retained in the output, affecting 18 out of 19 pages. There is also one MAJOR programmatic defect regarding a table caption coded as a header row. According to the strict routing rules, any critical defect automatically requires a REJECT decision. Additionally, the shingling recall (0.916) and word count ratio (0.774) reflect some discrepancies, although the LLM fidelity signals were seemingly unaware of the pervasive programmatic defects.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (35): 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report; 1; 2023 Roanoke Rapids Vegetation Survey Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=35, pages_affected=18/19. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 1 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=1. Examples: p6: "Roanoke Rapids". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://www.dominionenergy.com/projects-and-facilities/hydro']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Address the pervasive pipeline failure retaining header and footer artifacts across the document.
Fix table caption semantics by replacing single spanning header rows with <caption> elements.
Correct inline hyperlink extraction and table header markup.
